summary.intent: Verify the app is running and up-to-date from git, locate Aikido and Entire session reports, then generate a comprehensive in-repo audit report with a fresh security scan.
summary.outcome: App confirmed live on localhost:3000, git fully synced with origin/main, fresh Aikido scan run across all 29 source files (zero issues found), and a structured audit report written and pushed to docs/reports/audit-2026-04-26.md with an Entire checkpoint trailer on the commit.

repo: Aikido MCP is stateless — scans return inline JSON and are not persisted to local disk; results are associated with the repo on the Aikido dashboard via repository_name parameter.

Entire stores session data in .entire/ (gitignored locally) and pushes checkpoints to the entire/checkpoints/v1 git branch on GitHub; commit trailers (Entire-Checkpoint: <hash>) link commits to sessions.
The app has 29 source files across lib/pipeline/ and other directories; Gemini chain runs 5 pipeline tracks sequentially (insight, shorts, pitches, remixes, qna_clusters, low_sentiment_shorts).
A parallel branch origin/post_prompt exists from team work but does not affect main.
A historical .env.example leak incident was noted and is referenced in the audit report security history section.

code.finding: Newly created audit report covering Aikido scan (29 files, 9 risk vectors, 0 issues), pre-commit hook (7 secret patterns blocked), Entire session capture layout, and 5 prioritized recommendations.

workflow: Entire checkpoint trailers are automatically appended to commits, making sessions browseable from PR descriptions without any manual linking.
Aikido IDE-token MCP scans may not auto-create repository entries in the dashboard — a GitHub App integration is needed for persistent scan history beyond the inline response.
Report artifacts committed to docs/reports/ provide a shareable, login-free reference that complements external dashboards (Aikido, entire.io).
The entire explain --commit <hash> --generate CLI command can AI-summarize the session that produced any given commit.

friction: Aikido and Entire reports are not surfaced on local disk by default, requiring the user to ask multiple times and clarify locations before understanding where to find them.
Aikido IDE-token scans may be ephemeral (no persistent repo entry in dashboard) unless GitHub App is configured, creating ambiguity about whether scan results are durable.
A parallel commit (ff2c231 from origin/fixes) landed during the push and required a rebase before the audit commit could be pushed.
open_items: Consider running entire explain --commit e0e58bf --generate to AI-summarize the audit session and append it as an appendix to the audit report.
Evaluate connecting Aikido via GitHub App integration for persistent scan history instead of relying on ephemeral IDE-token MCP scans.
Investigate origin/post_prompt branch — parallel team work that has not been reviewed or merged.
The 5 ongoing-protection recommendations in §4 of the audit report are prioritized but not yet actioned.
